up.method: reported qualitatively; CH4 consumption rate stated to be 30-100x higher than any other reported methanotroph but no absolute value given

growth_records.cite: Stein et al. Microbial methane munchers offer a shield from the scorch. Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A (2023). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2313579120. [PMC10523581]
growth_records.snippet: the specific growth rate was proportional to the supplied CH4 concentration, meaning that it decreased to 0.004 from 0.07 cell divisions per hour when CH4 was supplied at 200 versus 2,500 ppm
growth_records.notes: SECONDHAND: value cited by this commentary FROM He et al. 2023 PNAS 'A methanotrophic bacterium to enable methane removal for climate mitigation' (PMC10466089, doi:10.1073/pnas.2310046120), NOT original to this paper. Reported growth = 0.07 'cell divisions per hour' at 2,500 ppm CH4. Treating 'cell divisions per hour' as doublings/h => doubling_time = 1/0.07 = 14.3 h. If instead interpreted as specific growth rate mu=0.07/h, td=ln2/0.07=9.9 h. No medium, temperature, pH, or absolute uptake/secretion rates reported. Formate excretion noted only relatively (~2x higher at 500 vs 1,000 ppm CH4); CH4 consumption noted only as 30-100x other methanotrophs; no absolute mmol/gDW/h values given.

up.method: reported qualitatively only; only methanotroph in collection sustaining growth at 200 ppm CH4

growth_records.cite: Stein et al. Microbial methane munchers offer a shield from the scorch. Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A (2023). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2313579120. [PMC10523581]
growth_records.snippet: it decreased to 0.004 from 0.07 cell divisions per hour when CH4 was supplied at 200 versus 2,500 ppm... nongrowth cell maintenance was estimated at ca. 25 to 35 times lower for M. buryatense 5GB1C grown at 200 versus 14,000 ppm CH4
growth_records.notes: SECONDHAND: value cited by this commentary FROM He et al. 2023 PNAS (PMC10466089, doi:10.1073/pnas.2310046120), NOT original to this paper. Reported growth = 0.004 'cell divisions per hour' at 200 ppm CH4. Treating as doublings/h => doubling_time = 1/0.004 = 250 h. If interpreted as mu=0.004/h, td=ln2/0.004=173 h. Commentary also states non-growth maintenance ATP ~25-35x lower at 200 vs 14,000 ppm CH4 (qualitative), validated with a genome-scale metabolic model of M. buryatense 5GB1C. No medium, temperature, pH, or absolute uptake/secretion rates given.

up.method: GC off-gas inlet-outlet species balance; specific via OD-to-CDW factor 0.201 gCDW/L/OD

yields.Yxs_note: derived Yxs = mu/qCH4 = 0.239/17.1 = 0.01398 gCDW per mmol CH4 (=13.98 gCDW/mol CH4)

growth_records.cite: Gilman, Alexey et al. Bioreactor performance parameters for an industrially-promising methanotroph Methylomicrobium buryatense 5GB1. Microbial Cell Factories (2015). DOI:10.1186/s12934-015-0372-8. [PMC4647623]
growth_records.snippet: M. buryatense 5GB1 grew faster on methane compared to methanol; mumax = 0.239 and 0.224 h-1 on methane. Specific methane uptake 17.1; Specific oxygen uptake 22.4 mmol gCDW-1 h-1; O2/CH4 uptake ratio 1.3 (Table 1, FM03)
growth_records.notes: Batch Table 1, expt FM03 (methane, fed-batch). Reported specific rates mmol gCDW-1 h-1: qCH4=17.1, qO2=22.4; absolute mmol/h: CH4=5.8, O2=7.6. mu=0.239 h-1 from OD exponential fit. Doubling time td=ln2/mu=0.693/0.239=2.90 h. Yxs derived = mu/qCH4 = 0.239/17.1 = 0.01398 gCDW/mmol CH4. Excreted formate/acetate/lactate reported as umol/gCDW accumulated at harvest (NOT per-time rates). Medium NMS2 composition not given in text (ref Puri et al. 2014). T=30C, pH 8.8 (2M NaOH control), agitation 1000 rpm, gas 100 sccm.

yields.Yxs_note: derived Yxs = mu/qCH4 = 0.224/19.8 = 0.01131 gCDW per mmol CH4

growth_records.cite: Gilman, Alexey et al. Bioreactor performance parameters for an industrially-promising methanotroph Methylomicrobium buryatense 5GB1. Microbial Cell Factories (2015). DOI:10.1186/s12934-015-0372-8. [PMC4647623]
growth_records.snippet: Unrestricted growth rate for M. buryatense 5GB1, mumax = 0.224 h-1. Specific methane uptake 19.8; specific oxygen uptake 24.7 mmol gCDW-1 h-1; O2/CH4 = 1.2 (Table 1, FM21)
growth_records.notes: Batch Table 1, expt FM21 (methane, fed-batch replicate). qCH4=19.8, qO2=24.7 mmol gCDW-1 h-1; absolute CH4=6.1, O2=7.6 mmol/h. mu=0.224 (Fig3 mumax=0.224). td=ln2/0.224=3.09 h. Yxs derived = 0.224/19.8 = 0.01131 gCDW/mmol CH4. Glycogen 2.7+/-1.1% CDW. Excreted acids are umol/gCDW accumulated (not per-time).

up.method: not measured (N/A in Table 1; liquid substrate, not off-gas)

growth_records.cite: Gilman, Alexey et al. Bioreactor performance parameters for an industrially-promising methanotroph Methylomicrobium buryatense 5GB1. Microbial Cell Factories (2015). DOI:10.1186/s12934-015-0372-8. [PMC4647623]
growth_records.snippet: When grown on methanol, M. buryatense 5GB1 excreted an unusually large amount of formate (as high as 13,486 umol gCDW-1)... Specific growth rate 0.169 h-1; specific oxygen uptake 11.3 mmol gCDW-1 h-1 (Table 1, FM18)
growth_records.notes: Batch Table 1, expt FM18 (methanol batch). qO2=11.3 mmol gCDW-1 h-1 (absolute 9.1 mmol/h). Methane/methanol specific uptake N/A (methanol is liquid, not tracked by off-gas). mu=0.169 h-1. td=ln2/0.169=4.10 h. Very high formate excretion 11,758 umol/gCDW accumulated (unbalanced growth on methanol). CDW 0.81 gCDW/L. Cannot derive Yxs (methanol qS not measured).

growth_records.cite: Gilman, Alexey et al. Bioreactor performance parameters for an industrially-promising methanotroph Methylomicrobium buryatense 5GB1. Microbial Cell Factories (2015). DOI:10.1186/s12934-015-0372-8. [PMC4647623]
growth_records.snippet: methanol-grown cells accumulated a large amount of glycogen (42.8 +/- 17.5 % of CDW)... formate as high as 13,486 umol gCDW-1. Specific growth rate 0.173 h-1; specific oxygen uptake 9.4 mmol gCDW-1 h-1 (Table 1, FM23)
growth_records.notes: Batch Table 1, expt FM23 (methanol batch replicate). qO2=9.4 mmol gCDW-1 h-1 (absolute 5.1 mmol/h). mu=0.173 h-1. td=ln2/0.173=4.01 h. Highest formate 13,486 umol/gCDW accumulated; glycogen 42.8+/-17.5% CDW (unbalanced growth). Methanol uptake not measured; Yxs not derivable.

yields.Yxs_note: derived Yxs = D/qCH4 = 0.119/9.7 = 0.01227 gCDW per mmol CH4

growth_records.cite: Gilman, Alexey et al. Bioreactor performance parameters for an industrially-promising methanotroph Methylomicrobium buryatense 5GB1. Microbial Cell Factories (2015). DOI:10.1186/s12934-015-0372-8. [PMC4647623]
growth_records.snippet: Continuous growth under O2 limitation. Dilution rate D = 0.122 h-1... FM50: D 0.119, cell density 0.79 gCDW/L, DO ~1%, specific methane uptake 9.7, specific oxygen uptake 11.1 mmol gCDW-1 h-1, CCE 46% (Table 2)
growth_records.notes: Continuous Table 2, expt FM50 (O2-limited chemostat). At steady state mu=D=0.119 h-1. td=ln2/0.119=5.82 h. qCH4=9.7, qO2=11.1 mmol gCDW-1 h-1 (absolute 7.7, 8.8 mmol/h). CDW 0.79 gCDW/L. Yxs derived = D/qCH4 = 0.119/9.7 = 0.01227 gCDW/mmol CH4. CCE 46%. Excreted acids umol/gCDW accumulated. O2 held ~1% saturation.

yields.Yxs_note: derived Yxs = D/qCH4 = 0.122/10.6 = 0.01151 gCDW per mmol CH4

growth_records.cite: Gilman, Alexey et al. Bioreactor performance parameters for an industrially-promising methanotroph Methylomicrobium buryatense 5GB1. Microbial Cell Factories (2015). DOI:10.1186/s12934-015-0372-8. [PMC4647623]
growth_records.snippet: FM51 (O2 limited): D 0.122 h-1, cell density 0.77 gCDW/L, DO ~1%, specific methane uptake 10.6, specific oxygen uptake 12.3 mmol gCDW-1 h-1, O2/CH4 1.2, CCE 43% (Table 2)
growth_records.notes: Continuous Table 2, expt FM51 (O2-limited chemostat replicate). mu=D=0.122 h-1. td=ln2/0.122=5.68 h. qCH4=10.6, qO2=12.3 mmol gCDW-1 h-1 (absolute 8.2, 9.5 mmol/h). CDW 0.77 gCDW/L. Yxs derived = 0.122/10.6 = 0.01151 gCDW/mmol CH4. CCE 43%, glycogen 13.1% CDW.

yields.Yxs_note: derived Yxs = D/qCH4 = 0.122/8.4 = 0.01452 gCDW per mmol CH4

growth_records.cite: Gilman, Alexey et al. Bioreactor performance parameters for an industrially-promising methanotroph Methylomicrobium buryatense 5GB1. Microbial Cell Factories (2015). DOI:10.1186/s12934-015-0372-8. [PMC4647623]
growth_records.snippet: Methane limitation... cell densities of 0.45 and 0.46 g CDW L-1 with dilution rates 0.122 and 0.126 h-1; DO 92%. FM38: specific methane uptake 8.4, specific oxygen uptake 13.7 mmol gCDW-1 h-1, O2/CH4 1.6, CCE 54% (Table 2)
growth_records.notes: Continuous Table 2, expt FM38 (methane-limited chemostat). mu=D=0.122 h-1. td=ln2/0.122=5.68 h. qCH4=8.4, qO2=13.7 mmol gCDW-1 h-1 (absolute 3.8, 6.2 mmol/h). CDW 0.45 gCDW/L, DO 92%. Yxs derived = 0.122/8.4 = 0.01452 gCDW/mmol CH4. CCE 54%. O2/CH4 ratio 1.6 (higher under CH4 limitation).

yields.Yxs_note: derived Yxs = D/qCH4 = 0.126/7.8 = 0.01615 gCDW per mmol CH4

growth_records.cite: Gilman, Alexey et al. Bioreactor performance parameters for an industrially-promising methanotroph Methylomicrobium buryatense 5GB1. Microbial Cell Factories (2015). DOI:10.1186/s12934-015-0372-8. [PMC4647623]
growth_records.snippet: FM39 (methane limited): D 0.126 h-1, cell density 0.46 gCDW/L, DO 92%, specific methane uptake 7.8, specific oxygen uptake 12.8 mmol gCDW-1 h-1, O2/CH4 1.6, CCE 61% (Table 2)
growth_records.notes: Continuous Table 2, expt FM39 (methane-limited chemostat replicate). mu=D=0.126 h-1. td=ln2/0.126=5.50 h. qCH4=7.8, qO2=12.8 mmol gCDW-1 h-1 (absolute 3.6, 5.9 mmol/h). CDW 0.46 gCDW/L, DO 92%. Yxs derived = 0.126/7.8 = 0.01615 gCDW/mmol CH4 (highest CCE 61%).

up.method: experimental value reported for M. buryatense 5GB1, cited from De la Torre et al. (2015)

growth_records.cite: Gupta, Ankit et al. Genome-scale metabolic reconstruction and metabolic versatility of an obligate methanotroph Methylococcus capsulatus str. Bath. PeerJ (2019). DOI:10.7717/peerj.6685. [PMC6613435]
growth_records.method: cited (growth ~0.23 h-1 associated with the 18.5 mmol/gDCW/h methane constraint from De la Torre et al., 2015); doubling time calculated
growth_records.snippet: we used the methane consumption rate of 18.5 mmol/gDCW/h, which has been reported for a related bacterium (Methylomicrobium buryatense 5GB1) (De la Torre et al., 2015), the growth rate was simulated to be 0.23 h-1 (similar to Mb5GB1)
growth_records.notes: Cited (not original) experimental datum: methane consumption 18.5 mmol/gDCW/h for M. buryatense 5GB1, originally from De la Torre et al. (2015). The associated growth ~0.23 h-1 is stated as 'similar to Mb5GB1'. td=ln2/0.23=0.6931/0.23=3.01 h. Recorded because it is an experimental prokaryote uptake rate surfaced in the paper; primary source = De la Torre et al. 2015. Medium/T/pH not given here.

growth_records.strain: 5GB1 (Delta glgA1 Delta glgA2 background; wild-type-lipid parent)
growth_records.medium: NMS2 defined medium (production formulation: 4x KNO3, 2x phosphate solution)

up.method: off-gas gas-sensor mass balance (BlueSens), Table 5 specific rate
maximum volumetric CH4 uptake rate, Table 5

maximum volumetric O2 uptake rate, Table 5

sec.method: off-gas mass balance, Table 5 specific CO2 evolution rate
maximum volumetric CO2 evolution rate, Table 5

growth_records.cite: Fei et al. Enhanced biological fixation of methane for microbial lipid production by recombinant Methylomicrobium buryatense. Biotechnology for Biofuels (2018). DOI:10.1186/s13068-018-1128-6. [PMC5934843]
growth_records.snippet: an OD of 55 with a maximum specific growth rate of 0.22 h-1 was observed during the batch cultures... Maximum O2 uptake rate 80.6, Maximum CH4 uptake rate 51.1, Maximum CO2 evolution rate 22.9 mmol/L/h; Specific O2 3.8, CH4 2.4, CO2 1.1 mmo
growth_records.notes: Values verbatim from Table 5 (high cell density culture, 900 ccm, 1000 rpm) and Table 4. mu=0.22 h-1 reported directly (batch, high cell density). Doubling time td=ln2/mu=0.693/0.22=3.15 h. Specific rates (mmol/gDCW/h) reported directly; volumetric maxima (mmol/L/h) reported directly. No Yxs stated. Obligate aerobic methanotroph oxidizing CH4->methanol->formaldehyde via RuMP cycle.

growth_records.strain: AP18 (5GB1C Delta glgA1 Delta glgA2 Delta sps; glycogen-abrogated mutant)
growth_records.medium: NMS2 defined medium (production formulation: 4x KNO3, 2x phosphate solution)

up.method: off-gas mass balance, Table 5 specific rate
maximum volumetric CH4 uptake rate, Table 5
off-gas mass balance, Table 5 specific rate
maximum volumetric O2 uptake rate, Table 5

sec.method: off-gas mass balance, Table 5 specific CO2 evolution rate
maximum volumetric CO2 evolution rate, Table 5

growth_records.cite: Fei et al. Enhanced biological fixation of methane for microbial lipid production by recombinant Methylomicrobium buryatense. Biotechnology for Biofuels (2018). DOI:10.1186/s13068-018-1128-6. [PMC5934843]
growth_records.snippet: AP18 reached stationary phase at a lower optical density and had a lower maximum growth rate compared with 5GB1... Specific O2 uptake rate 5.7, Specific CH4 uptake rate 3.5, Specific CO2 evolution rate 2.3 mmol/gDCW/h; Maximum O2 87.8, CH4
growth_records.notes: Values verbatim from Table 5 and Table 4. Text states AP18 had lower maximum growth rate than 5GB1 but no numeric mu given for AP18 (so growth_rate_per_h null; not fabricated). Higher CH4/O2 uptake and CO2 evolution than 5GB1 reflect more carbon/energy diverted to lipid, not glycogen (sps deletion abrogates glycogen). Lipid productivity 45.4 mg/L/h is paper's headline maximum.

growth_records.medium: not stated (industrial/defined medium in cited source)

up.method: not given as rate; only per-substrate yield reported

sec.method: volumetric productivity, cited in review from Henard et al. 2016 Sci Rep 6:21585

growth_records.cite: Jinyi Qian et al. Biological conversion of methane to organic molecules: towards a low-carbon bioeconomy. National Science Review (2025). DOI:10.1093/nsr/nwaf547. [PMC12857221]
growth_records.snippet: Lactic acid | M. buryatense 5GB1C | 0.8 g/L, 0.05 g/g CH4, 0.008 g/L/h | L-lactate dehydrogenase overexpression, Fermentation engineering | [108]
growth_records.notes: Secondhand value from review Table 4 (Qian et al. 2025). Primary source = ref [108] Henard CA, Smith H, Dowe N et al. Bioconversion of methane to lactate by an obligate methanotrophic bacterium. Sci Rep 2016;6:21585 (doi:10.1038/srep21585). Reported: titer 0.8 g/L, product yield 0.05 g lactate/g CH4, volumetric rate 0.008 g/L/h. No medium composition, temperature, pH, or growth rate given in the review. Methane uptake rate not stated numerically (only per-CH4 yield). Methylomicrobium buryatense 5GB1C is an aerobic Group I (gammaproteobacterial) methanotroph.

growth_records.medium: not stated (industrial medium in cited source)

sec.method: specific secretion rate, cited in review from Garg et al. (J Ind Microbiol Biotechnol)

growth_records.cite: Jinyi Qian et al. Biological conversion of methane to organic molecules: towards a low-carbon bioeconomy. National Science Review (2025). DOI:10.1093/nsr/nwaf547. [PMC12857221]
growth_records.snippet: Lactic acid | M. buryatense 5GB1C | 0.14 mmol/gDCW/h | A small-scale cultivation platform, Synthetic biology approach | [107]
growth_records.notes: Secondhand value from review Table 4. Primary source = ref [107] Garg S, Clomburg JM, Gonzalez R. A modular approach for high-flux lactic acid production from methane in an industrial medium using engineered Methylomicrobium buryatense 5GB1 (J Ind Microbiol Biotechnol). Reported specific lactate secretion rate 0.14 mmol/gDCW/h. No medium composition, temperature, pH, or growth rate in the review. Distinct from the ref-[108] 5GB1C record (different primary study, different rate units).

sec.method: specific secretion rate, continuous gas fermentation, cited in review from Henard et al. 2019 Green Chem 21:6731

growth_records.cite: Jinyi Qian et al. Biological conversion of methane to organic molecules: towards a low-carbon bioeconomy. National Science Review (2025). DOI:10.1093/nsr/nwaf547. [PMC12857221]
growth_records.snippet: Muconic acid | M. buryatense 5GB1C | 12.4 mg/L, 1.2 mg/g CH4, 0.023 mg/gDCW/h | GEM, Weakening the competing pathway, Continuous gas fermentation | [144]
growth_records.notes: Secondhand value from review Table 4. Primary source = ref [144] Henard CA, Akberdin IR, Kalyuzhnaya MG et al. Muconic acid production from methane using rationally-engineered methanotrophic biocatalysts. Green Chem 2019;21:6731-7 (doi:10.1039/C9GC03722E). Reported: titer 12.4 mg/L, yield 1.2 mg muconate/g CH4, specific rate 0.023 mg/gDCW/h. No medium, temperature, pH, or growth rate in review. Aerobic Group I methanotroph, GEM-guided strain.
